title: Tear down every long-lived subscription
description: A manual subscribe outlives the component unless unsubscribed, so prefer the async pipe or takeUntilDestroyed.
body: Use when a component or service subscribes to an observable that does not complete on its own (router events, stores, websockets, intervals). Do not call .subscribe() in ngOnInit and forget it. Actions: prefer binding the stream in the template with the async pipe so Angular manages teardown; for imperative cases use takeUntilDestroyed() or a destroy Subject completed in ngOnDestroy. Validation signals include a test that destroying the component stops further emissions and a lint/check that no bare subscribe lacks teardown. Risk boundary: leaked subscriptions accumulate across route navigations, causing memory growth and duplicate side effects; treat a long-lived bare subscribe as a defect.
